If you were ever in Japan, and were hoping to see anything that even closely resembles an average Japanese park, you will be deeply disappointed after visiting the Japanese Friendship Garden in San Jose. All the ponds are dry, not to mention no waterfalls or fish like in the pictures. Maintenance of the place is not great. Even that nice red bridge in the pictures, is blocked on both ends and so cannot be crossed (not to mention that it is much smaller than in looks in the pictures..). 
The gardens are not at all marked at the entrance to the parking lot (which only says Kelley Park), so it is easy to miss. Once you park there, you have to go around to the back side, in order to find the entrance. 
So if you are making a special trip to see this Japanese Friendship Garden - pass.
With the amount of taxes this city brings in there is no excuse to allow this beautiful park to be in such a state of disarray. We couldn’t be more disappointed. Shameful
Great small escape in the heart of silicon valley. After a bush day, why jot have a coffee and walk around such serene and peaceful place.  Very relaxing.
